programme.title: Rage at Dawn
programme.desc: In 1866, the Reno Brothers gang is ravaging Indiana. When a detective agency sends a man to infiltrate the gang by posing as an outlaw, he sets a trap to capture the gang during an early-morning train robbery.

programme.title: War of the Wildcats
programme.desc: During the early days of the Oklahoma oil boom, a greedy, self-assured oilman and a quiet, yet determined cowboy fight for the rights to oil on Indian land. All the while a pretty schoolteacher steals both men's hearts.

programme.title: Handcrafted America
programme.desc: Handcrafted America - Outdoor Furniture, Flutes and Handmade Paper: Host Jill Wagner travels the country on a quest to find talented craftsmen who still make incredible items by hand. Her first stop is Randleman, North Carolina, where James Broyhill, a woodworker with a legendary furniture lineage, shows her how to break down old whiskey barrels and turn them into rustic outdoor furniture. Then, Jill heads to Boston to visit a group of artisans who transform precious metals into some of the most sought after flutes in the world. Finally, Jill goes west to Portland, Oregon, where husband-and-wife team Jennifer and Ron Rich, repurpose old clothing rags into fine papers.

programme.title: Handcrafted America
programme.desc: Handcrafted America - Glass Chandeliers, Garden Tools, Western Saddles: Host Jill Wagner travels the country on a quest to find talented craftsmen who still make incredible items by hand. At her first stop in Scottsdale, Arizona, she meets Newt Grover, who taught himself to blow glass at 40. His beautiful, larger-than-life chandeliers prove it's never too late to become a master. Next, she meets Bob Denman in Boring, Oregon, who is one of the last craftsmen in the world making hand-forged iron garden tools. Finally, in Marysville, California, Jill meets a leather working contradiction in terms: he's a Frenchman, named Pedro Pedrini, who carves incredible Western saddles.

programme.title: Into The Wild Frontier
programme.desc: Into The Wild Frontier - Daniel Boone: Hero of Kentucky: After frontiersman Daniel Boone forges paths across the Appalachian Mountains and into the newly discovered lands of Kentucky, he founds the settlement of Boonesborough, which quickly becomes one of the most important outposts on colonial America's frontier. But this westward expansion comes at a price. Despite agreements made with the powerful Cherokee nation, conflict between the settlers and natives soon rages like wildfire. For Boone, the strife becomes intensely personal when his daughter and two other young women are abducted by Hanging Maw, a fearsome Cherokee chief. In a desperate chase, Boone pursues Hanging Maw's warriors and their captives through untamed wilderness, with his daughter's life hanging in the balance.

programme.title: Into The Wild Frontier
programme.desc: Into The Wild Frontier - Joe Walker: Humble Hero: Joe Walker doesn't head west to seek fame and fortune, but his trailblazing explorations across the uncharted frontier usher in a golden age of expansion and settlement that help create the United States as we know them today.

programme.title: Wild West Chronicles
programme.desc: Wild West Chronicles - Willie Kennard Makes His Mark in History: Under siege by gunslingers, the gold mining town of Yankee Hill runs a newspaper advertisement to find a new marshal. Former slave and Union soldier Willie Kennard answers the ad, but he faces prejudice and doubt as he works to restore law and order.

programme.title: Shaq's Garage
programme.desc: Shaq's Garage - The Last Of The Unicorns / Tiny Biggie D: After a fundraising picnic Dawkins, Boone, and Himari stay behind to study a rare flower. When Dawkins spots what he believes is a Unicorn he sets out to prove that they exist. When he comes back with proof it seems Keeter Rebus is up to his usual tricks. / When Biggie D and Himari secretly watch the testing of a shrink ray, the ray misses the target and hits Biggie D. On the way home Biggie D starts to rapidly shrink and starts to worry. Himari has to protect him from various threats until they can get Biggie D some help from the Paq.

programme.title: Bakugan Geogan Rising
programme.desc: Bakugan Geogan Rising - Gregorius Reed Arrives / A Mysterious Bakugan: Drago receives a strange message from Pyravian about a stolen core cell. / Drago and the Awesome Brawlers track the missing core cell to the estate of a wealthy Bakugan collector, Gregorious Reed.

programme.title: Bakugan Geogan Rising
programme.desc: Bakugan Geogan Rising - The Search for Geogan! / The Geogan Arcleon!: In search of the mysterious Geogan they witnessed the night before, the AB have a run in with Crystal Blue, Everett and Strata. / An AAAnimus Security Force transport vehicle loaded with confiscated Baku-Balls is hijacked.

programme.title: Super Mario Bros 3
programme.desc: Super Mario Bros 3 - Toadally Magical Adventure: Toad loses a magic wand to the Koopas. / When the Mario Brothers go back to Brooklyn, they are mistaken as babysitters to look after Junior. Mario & Luigi must control and get Junior back home.

programme.title: Super Mario Bros 3
programme.desc: Super Mario Bros 3 - Oh Brother / Mighty Plumber: When Mario and Luigi get fed up with each other, Luigi soon finds himself having to save his brother from Koopa and Kooky. / Koopa brings TV hero Mighty Plumber to life and tricks him into robbing the Pipe Land Treasury.

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh!

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh!
programme.desc: Yu-Gi-Oh! - Mechanical Mayhem, Part 1: Big 4 Nezbitt challenges Duke, Tristan, and Serenity for the right to gain control of three bodies at once!

programme.title: Shaq's Garage
programme.desc: Shaq's Garage - Super Gronk / Bates Bumpy Day: Gronk decides that he wants to be a superhero, just like Shaq was in a movie the Paq watched. While patrolling the city, Gronk accidently causes more problems than he solves. When Keeter tries to ruin a parade that Shaq is participating in, Gronk must step in to save the day. / When a Pablo wins the chance at a day with a member of the Shaq Paq, Bates steps up to give him a day of fun. After showing Pablo some of his own favorite things, Bates has to step out of his comfort zone in order to ensure the winner has a good day.

programme.title: Shaq's Garage
programme.desc: Shaq's Garage - Runaway Train / New Friends: While Dr. Grease is inspecting an old steam locomotive, it takes off by itself down the track. Biggie D, Boone, Fleming, and Himari have to band together in order to overcome obstacles to get ahead of the runaway train and bring it to a stop. / New members Curl, Gonzales, and Layla get introduced and the Paq befriend them. While Bates, Himari, and Klaus are spending time with the new members, they find it difficult to relate to the new cars. The Paq must put aside their differences and work together in order to save Shaq from danger.

programme.title: Shaq's Garage
programme.desc: Shaq's Garage - Pilot / The Contest: The Shaq Paq is a group of sentient cars, led by Shaquille O'Neal, that helps out around the city of Orlanta. When a mother loses control of her baby stroller the Paq must band together to keep the baby from harm and return them to their mother. / Shaq enters Biggie D into a truck pull contest. The rest of the Shaq Paq helps him train but shockingly, Biggie D is defeated by another truck! That's when Dawkins realizes something doesn't quite add up. Shaq and the Paq band together to set things straight and learn an important lesson about fair play..

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.sub-title: Lullaby Time: Soothing Sounds for Baby
programme.desc: One of the greatest joys you'll ever experience is bonding with your baby during those calm, quiet moments. Lullaby Time: Soothing Sounds for Baby provides comforting sights and sounds as you relax and share this precious downtime.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: At 12 months, babies can identify specific sounds, and they re also starting to match the sounds they hear with the objects that make them. Meet the Orchestra -- First Instruments presents your little "maestro" with a playful introduction to the sights and sounds of a real orchestra -- from the clashing of cymbals to the purring of the cello and everything in between!

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: As little ones become better acquainted with the world, they grow curious about animals of all kinds, beyond just the family pet. And as their communication skills continue to develop, they enjoy imitating animal sounds as well. Baby Noah Animal Expedition takes your little explorer across the globe to meet animals -- two-by-two -- from the savannah, the rainforest and tropics, the Outback, the ocean and the Polar Regions.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: Learn all about music and rhythm in the world around us! Discover classical music and learn instrument names through narration and repetition. Inspire sweet dreams with gentle melodies and a comforting bedtime story. Learn about classical music from Beethoven, Bach, and Mozart. Sing and dance along to classic rhythmic songs like The Wheels on the Bus and see real-life images of instruments.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: World Music introduces you and your little one to beautiful sounds from every continent, bringing a fresh, new experience of music and culture. Sing, dance and play to exotic rhythms that open the door to an appreciation of different cultures from around the world.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: As babies grow and change, so does the landscape around them. From fiery red leaves to falling snowflakes from flowers in bloom to fireworks in July, every season of the year has its own special magic just waiting to be discovered.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: Dive in with your little explorer across the seas to meet amazing animals living in the ocean, coral reef, and polar regions. Overflowing with underwater footage of whales, dolphins, tropical fish, and more, Neptune's Oceans invites you and your baby on an oceanic voyage of discovery!

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.sub-title: Baby's Favorite Places: First Words Around Town
programme.desc: As babies turn one year old, they continue to develop their communication skills. Meanwhile, with you as their guide, they also begin to explore the world beyond their front door. Baby's Favorite Places presents them with a playful introduction to 20 words for the names of familiar locations from the market, to the library, to the local park.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: Baby's innate curiosity about all they see and hear increases on a daily basis. Before long, they begin to associate words with objects and to imitate and articulate sounds. Why not give their eyes and ears something wonderful to feast upon? Baby Shakespeare presents little ones with 12 common words that can be found within the rich context of classic poems and the beauty of nature.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.sub-title: Discovering Shapes: Circles, Squares, and More
programme.desc: As babies develop, their understanding of the world, and how its pieces "fit" together, continues to progress. DISCOVERING SHAPES playfully introduces five basic forms circle, oval, triangle, square and rectangle as each appears in the context of toys, nature and everyday objects.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.sub-title: On the Go: Riding, Sailing and Soaring
programme.desc: As little ones become increasingly mobile, their fascination with all things that move intensifies too. On the Go: Riding, Sailing and Soaring exposes babies to the different ways people travel -- on land, by sea, and in the air -- while it presents them with captivating real-world images, funny puppet shows, and beautiful classical music by Beethoven, Haydn, Mozart and Rossini.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: To convince the dance-averse Huckleberry to attend the big masquerade ball at the dog park, Blueberry promises she'll stay by his side the whole evening and make sure he doesn't have to dance. But as the day of the ball dawns, Blueberry awakens with a bad cold. She doesn't know how to tell Huck that she can't go to the ball. Lemon and Raspberry misinterpret her sniffles, thinking she's so heartbroken to be disappointing Huck that she's crying!

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: When a comedy of errors causes Orange, Plum and Cherry to arrive late to their campout, they start off their annual tradition of telling tall-tales around the campfire.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Lemon and Raspberry's fashion podcast is saved by Apple, then almost sabotaged by Sweet and Sour when Lemon's "mood hair" runs amok, acting like the bickering twins, and the twins' comical argument is accidentally broadcast on the "berrynet."

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: All of Berry Bitty City is excitedly making their way through the berry batch in search of the elusive Wanderberry. It is to be the dessert at the BerryFest Feast. Everyone is milling about searching for it on their own when Blueberry points out that the rules say the BerryFest Princess is in charge of the search. The girls turn to her for direction and she points them where she thinks the berry might be.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.sub-title: The Berry Best You Can Bee
programme.desc: Strawberry is at Orange's store to mail a birthday card to a friend in Big Bitty City. Just after she drops the card into the mail slot, an ill postal carrier bee sneezes sending envelopes and post cards flying in all directions. The two girls help Jadeybug, a postal clerk, and Postmaster Bee gather up the strewn letters and the ill bee gets sent home from work.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Strawberry opens her front door to a throng of Berrykins all cheering for the new BerryFest Princess. She is appreciative of their admiration but unsure of exactly what her duties entail. In response the Berrykins bombard her with her duties - major to miniscule. One Berrykin sums it up by telling her she's responsible for presiding over all the activities of the BerryFest to make it the best.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Apple Dumplin' wants to help Strawberry & her Friends with the baking. But Dumplin's just a little too little to be much help. In fact, she ends up making a pretty big mess of things.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Angel Cake likes everything "just so" - and when things don't go her way, she's a pretty bad sport... until Strawberry & Apple Dumplin' help her become the berry best of sports.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: At the urging of Honey Pie Pony, Strawberry & Friends decide to put on a Cooking Show. Ginger Snap invents a version of TV, which she calls Berryvision, to broadcast the show all over Strawberryland.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Annie Oatmeal hires both Strawberry Shortcake and Angel Cake to work at her dude ranch. Things get complicated when there's a competition to decide which Ranch Hand runs the Best Bunkhouse.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: One night, as Strawberry & Friends gaze at the stars, they talk about what they want to be when they grow up.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: When Strawberryland's one movie theatre goes out of business, Strawberry has an idea to save it: she and her friends can film a movie to show there and attract crowds back to the theatre.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Strawberry and her friends love to dance - and the Peculiar Purple Pieman of Porcupine Peak is taking advantage of that!

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: When Strawberry & her friends decide to put on a talent show, they plan to enter themselves as a band, The Strawberry Jams!

programme.title: Blippi's Alphabet and Transportation Adventures
programme.desc: Join Blippi on a series of exciting educational journeys where he uncovers the Case of the Missing ABCs, explores the charming Ranch at the Pier, and delves into London's rich transportation history at both the Transport Museum and the RAF Museum. From solving puzzles and meeting farm animals to discovering vintage buses and historic airplanes, Blippi makes learning an adventure filled with curiosity and fun.

programme.title: Blippi's Fun and Colorful Exploration
programme.desc: Get ready for a vibrant and entertaining escapade with Blippi as he dives into a world of colors at Billy Beez, creates a whimsical pizza in the kitchen, transforms into a playful pirate on a treasure hunt, and explores the fascinating world of dinosaurs at the Santa Barbara Museum of Natural History. Each adventure is packed with laughter, creativity, and educational moments, making learning a joyful experience for kids.

programme.title: The Berry Big Harvest
programme.desc: Finding she has some free time one morning, Strawberry visits Berrykin Bloom who is filled with excitement over his new crop of Berry Bitty fruits and vegetables his garden has never grown so much produce! He can't wait to share his new crop (the perfect bitty size) with Berry Bitty City, so Strawberry volunteers to help him transport the produce to Orange's General Store. Soon Orange's store is overflowing with Berry Bitty Fruits & Veggies. It's the berry first time Orange has not been able to find a “place for everything” in her store.

programme.title: Room at the Top
programme.desc: Strawberry is really touched when her girlfriends and the Berrykins volunteer to help her with the construction of her new marketplace, so when the girls suggest they take charge of designing Strawberry's dream-bedroom to go on the second story, of course Strawberry agrees. While construction is going on, the girls each show Strawberry their individual ideas for the bedroom. Grateful for the support and not wanting to dampen anyone's enthusiasm, Strawberry tells each of them yes (actually, more like not-no).

programme.title: All My Senses
programme.desc: Sid wants to know about all his different senses. At school, Sid and his friends embark on a quest to explore vision, hearing, touch, smell, and taste!

programme.title: A Brush with Teeth
programme.desc: At school, Sid discovers that our mouths are filled with different kinds of teeth that do lots of different jobs--like chewing our food! So it's really important to keep our teeth healthy by brushing them every day!

programme.title: Sky's the Limit
programme.desc: When a storm causes a giant boulder to cut off Berry Bitty City's water supply, Strawberry and the girls face the possibility of having to move away from their homes. With the help of Mr. Longface Caterpillar, the girls set out on an adventure to save their town.

programme.title: Tarzan and Jane
programme.desc: After returning to London, Tarzan and Jane encounter Jane's mother, Angela, a celebrity-cum-journalist who is researching an expose claiming Greystoke Industries harms the environment. The truth is someone is trying to destroy Greystoke Industries. Tarzan and Jane must figure out who this mystery saboteur is while battling fires, snakes, and sneak attacks. With this Mastermind's henchmen trying to stop them at every turn, our young heroes must take part in a thrilling, multi-country adventure in order to save Greystoke Industries and finally put an end to the criminal enterprise that threatens to put the environment and countless lives - human and animal alike - in danger!

programme.title: World Bear Day Specials
programme.desc: Lemur's teddy bear Bobo is feeling unwell. Can Doctor Fluffy save the day?

programme.title: World Bear Day Specials
programme.desc: Counting with Paula is a fun and educational series about numbers for children.

programme.title: Storytime
programme.desc: Join Mamma O and Baby O every day to hear a fabulous story!

programme.title: Leo The Wildlife Ranger
programme.desc: Leo, Katie, Hero and a sun bear cub set off for the rainforest to find the cub's mother.

programme.title: Where's This From?
programme.desc: Can you help Flash work out where these animals come from?

programme.title: ZooMoo
programme.desc: The ZooMoo team learn about bears.

programme.title: World Bear Day Specials
programme.desc: Let's go salmon fishing with a bear!

programme.title: Animal Show With Stinky And Jake
programme.desc: Stinky and Jake welcome a very special guest Jake to the show.

programme.title: World Bear Day Specials
programme.desc: Sleeping Baby O is woken by a different animal noise coming from outside.

programme.title: Splash And Bubbles
programme.desc: Dunk's cousin Mo comes to visit everyone in the reef.

programme.title: Splash And Bubbles
programme.desc: Gush travels with the kids to the Arctic Ocean / A great white shark has a toothache.

programme.title: World Bear Day Specials
programme.desc: Join us and test your animal knowledge on ZooMoo's Got Brains a game show like no other!

programme.title: The Final Game
programme.desc: With the fate of the dragon horns hanging in the balance, the Ninja must rally to defend them from those who seek their power.

programme.title: Elements of Betrayal
programme.desc: The Ninja clash with the notorious Forbidden Five in a fierce showdown, but the arrival of unexpected allies shifts the momentum.

programme.title: Prophecy Of The Green Ninja, Greatest Battles
programme.desc: So the final puzzle forces ninja to question the true meaning of the Green Ninja Prophecy. Solving the puzzles releases a new peril for the Ninja to face. Will memories of their greatest victories be enough to defeat it?

programme.title: The Power and the Peril!
programme.desc: Rock star mathematician Ian Malcolm returns to Isla Nublar and helps Claire, Owen and Hudson deal with strange power surges all over Jurassic World.

programme.title: Fistful of Doppelgangers
programme.desc: The Candy Realm is under siege, and the dream chasers are tasked with saving its sugary inhabitants. As they orchestrate a mass evacuation, Mateo and Astrid sneak into the Midnight Raven.

programme.title: Irrational Treasure
programme.desc: The elusive Eye of Confusion has been located, but the dream chasers are not alone in their pursuit. With the Never Witch's doppelgangers on their heels, the team embarks on a frantic treasure hunt.

programme.title: Mission Inplausible
programme.desc: As the Eye of Confusion's power threatens to unravel reality, Logan and Astrid are forced to confront their deepest fears in the Dream Forge.

programme.title: Music “Battle of the Stars”
programme.desc: Nova pulls off a series of pranks, leaving her friends scrambling and laughing. However, things take a dark turn when one of her pranks goes awry and sends the group into a terrifying situation.

programme.title: The Royal Blacksmiths
programme.desc: The four ninja go undercover as a dance troupe and enter a talent contest in their quest to win “The Blade Cup” where one of the fang blades is hidden.

programme.title: Marvel Superheroes
programme.desc: Loki arrives at Avengers Tower declaring he wants to be an Avenger. Our heroes are skeptical, but Iron Man decides to make Loki an ”Avenger In Training”. It's a great plan until Thanos arrives on Earth looking for Loki

programme.title: The Book Of Total Badness
programme.desc: The NEXO-Knights must fight Jestro's Monsters of Destruction which are attacking all over the Kingdom. But Merlok smells a trap…is he right? In a dark, destroyed part of the kingdom, Jestro arrives at the ruins of a HUGE castle. The Book of Monsters knows this place: the great evil wizard Monstrux lived here. The Book sniffs out some powerful magic in the ruins: “The Book of Destruction.” With this, The Book tells Jestro they can destroy the NEXO-Knights and then attack the King's Castle and cause massive destruction. Jestro creates destructive monsters and sends them all over the realm, so the Knights must separate and fight single combats all over the place. The Knights lose these battles (lesson: they have to work as a team!) And, just when the Knights are scattered across the Kingdom, Jestro attacks the King's Castle! It looks like all is lost. When Aaron, who has escaped, returns to see Jestro's attack, he realizes he must gather his fellow Knights. Merlok says it's very dangerous, but Aaron says he must take the risk to gather the Knights and save Knighton.

programme.title: Gorilla's Gone Wild
programme.desc: Cragger angers the Gorilla tribe and they march to the Crocodile Swamp to teach him a lesson. But the Gorilla Mechs are not fit for the swampy water and the Lions and Eagles must come to the rescue. The only problem is that the Lions don't like water ... at all!

programme.title: The Haunted and the Hunted!
programme.desc: Something mysterious lurking the jungle wrecks a construction site, and Hudson is convinced a ghost is responsible - but it might be something even more surprising.

programme.title: The Snowball Effect
programme.desc: Laval and Li'Ella's relationship grows as he gives her a tour of the Lion City. But distracted, he misses a key battle against the Hunters. Tormak joins the fight and saves the day. But the Lion King Lagravis is captured and brought to the Ice Fort. Who will lead the Lions now?!?

programme.title: The Final Game
programme.desc: With the fate of the dragon horns hanging in the balance, the Ninja must rally to defend them from those who seek their power.

programme.title: Elements of Betrayal
programme.desc: The Ninja clash with the notorious Forbidden Five in a fierce showdown, but the arrival of unexpected allies shifts the momentum.

programme.title: Pokémon
programme.desc: Ash, Brock, and Misty get sidetracked on their way to Viridian City when all of their belongings mysteriously disappear. While searching for their missing things, they find themselves mixed up in a close encounter that's really out of this world!

programme.title: Pokémon
programme.desc: Our heroes make their way to Viridian City, where Ash hopes to earn the Earth Badge. They get to the Gym just in time to see his rival, Gary, being defeated by an unknown Pokémon—and Gary insists that it's evil!

programme.title: Pokémon
programme.desc: On their way back to Pallet Town, our heroes encounter a runaway Mr. Mime. Ash disguises himself as another Mr. Mime so he can get close. When Team Rocket strikes, will they catch the right Mr. Mime?

programme.title: Pokémon
programme.desc: Our heroes find themselves at a fork in the road and decide to climb a steep and dangerous mountain. Pikachu gets lost, and they must all work together to find it, stay warm, and weather a storm.

programme.title: Pokémon
programme.desc: On a visit to Professor Oak's house, Ash runs into Gary, and they talk about the upcoming Pokémon League. Professor Oak says they should learn from each other rather than competing, and that teamwork pays off when Team Rocket attacks!

programme.title: Pokémon
programme.desc: Ash joins his mom for a vacation on Seafoam Island. While there, he seeks out the programmer of the Pokédex to learn about the unusual Evolution of SlowPoké. This mystery is surprisingly revealed by Team Rocket's bumbling!

programme.title: Pokémon
programme.desc: When Ash wipes out during a surfing attempt, Victor rescues him and tells him about Humungadunga, a giant wave that sweeps through every 20 years. Can Ash and Pikachu give Victor the confidence he needs to catch the giant wave?

programme.title: Pokémon
programme.desc: Back in Pallet Town, Ash and friends meet Florinda, who works at the local nursery. Her Gloom won't evolve, so she's afraid she's not training it properly. When Team Rocket attacks, everyone sees just how strong a Pokémon can become!

programme.title: Pokémon
programme.desc: A famous director comes to town, and everybody wants to be the star! Since he only wants Pokémon in his movie, Ash, Brock, Misty, and Team Rocket fight hard to make sure it's their Pokémon that will star opposite Wigglytuff.

programme.title: Pokémon
programme.desc: When the gang attends the premiere of "Pokémon in Love," Meowth looks back on his painful past. He shares the story of his life before Team Rocket, including how he learned to walk and talk like a human.

programme.title: Pokémon
programme.desc: With only three weeks left before the Pokémon League competition, Ash, Brock, and Misty head up to Mt. Hideaway to find one of the Elite Four. They're after Pokémon training tips, but they discover a land of overgrown Onix instead!

programme.title: Pokémon
programme.desc: While Ash and Brock train for the Pokémon League, they uncover a hidden temple containing Pokémon fossils. Misty remembers a hidden city where people built temples to honor Pokémon. Could they have stumbled upon the ancient city of Pokémopolis?

programme.title: Pokémon
programme.desc: Our heroes are headed to the Pokémon League tournament at the Indigo Plateau when they meet another Trainer going their way. Unfortunately, all of his Gym badges have been stolen, so he can't compete...unless he takes Ash's badges instead!

programme.desc: LankyBox is Roblox superstars Justin and Adam, two best friends who love playing popular video games!

programme.title: Awesome Adventures!
programme.desc: Travel the world, zipline through jungles, hike mountain tops and find treasure in the tropics. The Ninja Family travel to Africa and visit a village, meet locals and explore the jungle.

programme.desc: The Ninja Kidz go head-to-head in fun physical competitions, gymnastic events and games. Electric unicycles vs. one wheels vs. hoverboards and a twin yoga challenge.

programme.title: Ninja Challenge
programme.desc: The Ninja Kidz go head-to-head in fun physical competitions, gymnastic events and games. Friends apply makeup blindfolded, play hide & seek, and complete a Ninja obstacle course.

programme.title: Ninja Prime
programme.desc: New premieres of the latest breaking releases and exclusive new videos from the Ninja Kidz Family. A very special behind the scenes look at the Ninja Family, with some special secrets revealed.

programme.title: Ninja Prime
programme.desc: New premieres of the latest breaking releases and exclusive new videos from the Ninja Kidz Family. The Ninja Kidz train for a wild battle, learn new ninja powers, and appear in a new music video.

programme.title: Ninja Universe
programme.desc: Bryton uses his super ninja skills as Spiderman and shows off parkour prowess.

programme.title: Ninja Universe
programme.desc: The Ninja Family bundles up for a visit to an ice fort for a special family rendition of Freeze Tag.

programme.title: Awesome Adventures!
programme.desc: Travel the world, zipline through jungles, hike mountain tops and find treasure in the tropics. Exploring a volcano lava tube, flipping in a jungle water park, and visiting the Colosseum in Rome.

programme.title: Awesome Adventures!
programme.desc: Travel the world, zipline through jungles, hike mountain tops and find treasure in the tropics. The Ninja Family explores a secret jungle path, discovering a hidden water park with waterfalls.

programme.title: Ninja Challenge
programme.desc: The Ninja Kidz go head-to-head in fun physical competitions, gymnastic events and games. A twin yoga challenge in the desert and a Las Vegas gymnastics meet.

programme.title: Ninja Challenge
programme.desc: The Ninja Kidz go head-to-head in fun physical competitions, gymnastic events and games. Ninja friends dare each other in a crazy game of "What Are the Odds" and a Sumo food challenge.

programme.title: Pocoyo
programme.desc: Weird things are happening to Pato: his hose won't work and his favourite hat seems to have a mind of its own. Pato investigates and finds Nina, a tiny girl dressed in green who lives among the plants with her robot Roberto. Pato, you've made a new friend! / Elly has a new house and wants to paint it pink. She asks Pocoyo and Pato for help, but they want to paint it a different colour. Teamwork involves compromise! They'll find a way to paint it a colour they all like, that's for sure.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Riley from Hollywood is directing his first action film. Jett brings an alien mask and ends up acting as the monster.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Callum from Scotland wants bagpipes that blow bubbles so he can play them for Nessie, the Loch Ness Monster.

programme.title: Wes Tank's StoryRaps
programme.desc: Wes raps another challenging tongue twister and shows us how hip hop beats are made! / Wes puts a new spin on the classic song "The Wheels on the Bus". / Wes raps the story of DJ Schooli Squrriel, a lil' girl who believes she can be a hip-hop superstar. (S1 E21)

programme.title: Wes Tank's StoryRaps
programme.desc: Wes raps Edward Lear's classic nursery rhyme "A Nonsense Alphabet". (S1 E01)

programme.title: Canticos (Español)
programme.desc: Disfruta de esta compilación de Canticos, "Celebremos el Verano (Bilingüe)", con las canciones "3 Pecesitos", "Marinero" y "El Barquito Chiquitito". ¡Perfecto para que tus hijos aprendan inglés mientras se divierten cantando y celebrando el verano! / Disfruta esta compilación de Canticos: "Canciones con Pin Pon (Bilingüe)", con las canciones "Pin Pon", "Cabeza, Hombros, Rodillas y Pies" y "Juguemos en el Bosque". / Disfruta de esta compilación de Canticos, "Los Pollitos lo pueden todo! (Español)". (S1 E103)

programme.title: Tic Tac Toy
programme.desc: The pretend Toys R Us workers are back and pranking each other as usual!

programme.title: The Bumble Nums
programme.desc: Once upon a time, Stumble, Humble, and Grumble set out on a journey to each build a kitchen of their very own. The Bumble Nums used different materials for their kitchens and were busy cooking when a big, bad visitor showed up. He threatened to huff and puff and blow their kitchens in. Can he do it? Or can the little chefs cook their way out of trouble using the secret ingredient? / Today, the Bumble Nums are going to make Yawning Yam Yogurt. But can Humble, Grumble, and Stumble get the secret ingredient from the garden without falling asleep? And will they ever get back to the kitchen in time for the Cooking Countdown? Watch and find out!

programme.title: The Bumble Nums
programme.desc: Today, the Bumble Nums are going to make Peek-a-boo Pecan Pie! How can Humble, Grumble, and Stumble get all the hiding peek-a-boo pecans they need for their tasty pies? programme.title: Mr. D
programme.desc: Gerry's been given another coaching assignment, but this time he's helping Ricki prepare for a fencing tournament, and he couldn't be less interested. After an uncharacteristic heart to heart, however, Gerry changes his tunes and assures Ricki that he'll get her to the tournament – a task that proves more difficult than he had anticipated. In an effort to capitalize on Dwyer's recent divorce, Bobbi invites him over for dinner and attempts to seduce him the only way she knows how. Poorly. Robert, meanwhile, is consumed with uncovering his lunch thief.

programme.title: Mr. D
programme.desc: When he attempts to reign in a bratty student, Gerry is faced with the wrath of his notoriously overprotective and obnoxious mother. After an innocent interaction with her takes an unpleasant and threatening turn, Gerry goes to great lengths to protect his reputation. Upon realizing that she has fallen into the role of staff “punching bag,” Bobbi attempts to transfer the title onto another staff member, but her awkward and cruel efforts only further seal her title. Meanwhile, Ricki sells pencils to Robert and drives a hard bargain.

programme.title: Mr. D
programme.desc: Gerry accompanies students on a leadership retreat to a nature centre where he meets the beautiful staff member, Jasmine. As Gerry and Robert engage in a misguided competition to win her affection, the two find themselves lost in the woods and in pretty dire straights. Meanwhile, when Lisa finds out that Trudy is running a morbid “death pool” gambling ring, she attempts to shut it down, only to learn that she's the odds on favourite to die next. And Simon and Frank's innocent game of rugby spoons takes an ugly turn.

programme.title: Mr. D
programme.desc: Gerry is turning forty, and after finding a letter he wrote to himself ten years earlier, he becomes acutely aware of everything he has failed to accomplish. Clearly not the family man, Gerry attempts to prove that he's the other type of “awesome forty.” But when his wild night out doesn't go as planned, Gerry takes drastic measures to meet the expectations set out in his own letter. Meanwhile, Simon's ferret goes missing and Lisa wants to impress the chairman of the board of trustees by putting on an elaborate fun fair in his honour, which quickly devolves into a complete disaster.

programme.title: Mr. D
programme.desc: After waking up married and quite hung-over, Gerry and Lisa do their best to face the workday. They quickly realize, however, that this ‘accidental marriage' thing is getting in the way and they set off to get an annulment as soon as possible. Meanwhile, Nisha accepts an invitation to have dinner with Simon and his mother in the hopes of mending fences. But when Angela's date shows up, bearing an uncanny resemblance to Simon, it becomes clear that Angela's acceptance of Nisha doesn't come without strings. Ms. Terdie feels redundant when she learns that Robert has ordered e-readers for the students.

programme.title: Mr. D
programme.desc: As Gerry begins the process of moving in to Lisa's apartment, Lisa finds herself deeply regretting her decision to give the marriage a shot. Meanwhile, in an effort to get in shape and lower his stress levels, Robert gets himself a treadmill desk. Trudy explains that Robert's new attire combined with the full-body sweat and disturbing ‘workout' noises emanating from his office are unprofessional and disruptive, but her words land on deaf ears. programme.title: ALF
programme.desc: There's a power blackout, so ALF is passing the time by watching the Tanners' neighbors, the Ochmoneks, who are fighting. By dinner time, the power has come back so the Tanners can have dinner. Then comes another, short blackout. Trevor Ochmonek appears at the Tanners' door...

programme.title: ALF

programme.title: ALF

programme.title: ALF
programme.desc: When the living room TV breaks down, Willie declares that he won't have it fixed, despite ALF's complaints that he has nothing to do but watch TV. ALF keeps pressuring Willie, who discovers that the TV is still under warranty and he can have it sent to the factory in Libya...

programme.title: ALF
programme.desc: An insect from Melmac has an unexpected reaction when exposed to earthly chemicals.

programme.title: ALF

programme.title: ALF
programme.sub-title: Working My Way Back to You

programme.title: ALF

programme.title: ALF

programme.title: ALF
programme.desc: Halloween is approaching and ALF wants to go trick-or-treating. Kate, of course, objects, but ALF persists. He argues that he has a natural costume and tries offering Willie a deal he can't refuse... Lynn presents Lizard to her parents.

programme.desc: ALF learns the concept of Earthly beauty pageant. Since Lynn, having been dumped, is suffering from low self-esteem, ALF enters her in Miss Southland pageant to prove she is pretty and uses the prizes to lure Lynn to participate.

programme.desc: Kate's mother has decided to get married in the Tanners' house. Unfortunately, ALF develops a Melmacian Hiccup, that keeps getting louder and louder, threatening to draw everyone's attention within a 100 meters (give or take).

programme.desc: To save his best friend's marriage, a lawyer seduces the man's mistress, not realizing it is the wrong woman.

programme.title: The Fuller Brush Girl
programme.desc: A door-to-door cosmetics saleswoman and her bumbling file clerk fiancé become prime suspects in a murder.

programme.title: Don't Raise The Bridge, Lower The River
programme.desc: A schemer hatches a plan to convert a swinging Chinese discotheque back into his wife's ancestral home.

programme.title: Hit By Lightning
programme.desc: The loneliest man in North America finally meets the perfect woman but there's only one catch - she's married and wants him to kill her husband.

programme.title: London Mitchell's Christmas
programme.desc: London becomes faced with a diagnosis of prostate cancer which forces him to rethink his total outlook on life during the Christmas holiday.

programme.title: Dear Santa
programme.desc: Crystal comes from a wealthy family and loves spending her parent's money. When they decide it's time for her to make something of her life they give her until Christmas to clean up her act.

programme.title: 12 Days of Christmas
programme.desc: During their first winter break from college, two childhood friends navigate turbulent reunions, unspoken romances, and even an unplanned pregnancy.

programme.title: The Last 24 Hours
programme.desc: This documentary offers an in-depth look at Jimi Hendrix's last 24 hours and attempts to clear the air surrounding his tragic passing by including appearances and testimonials from some of the people that knew him best. Directed by Mike Parkinson.

programme.title: The Last 24 Hours
programme.desc: The world mourned the loss of one of it's most innovative performers in April 2016. While Prince's official autopsy confirmed that the singer died from an overdose of the painkiller fentanyl, investigators have still been looking to clear up some additional mysteries surrounding the case. We take a look into the last 24 hours of his death and look for the real answers as to what happened. Directed by Jordan Hill.

programme.title: The Last 24 Hours
programme.desc: The ultimate king of pop Michael Jackson spent his last hours battling insomnia and pleading with his personal physician to give him a powerful anesthetic to combat his chronic sleeplessness according to a search-warrant affidavit. We explore the truth behind this and discover the real facts, with exclusive footage and testimonials. Directed by Jordan Hill.

programme.title: Leguizamo Does America
programme.desc: Though often overlooked, Latinos have been making contributions to the culture of NYC all along. Whether through food, dance, music or fashion, Latinos have helped make this city what it is: A cultural hub that the world looks to for inspiration. John begins his journey in Jackson Heights, Queens, the borough that shaped him as a man and as an artist, where he highlights the Latino spot that helps make every borough feel like home…the Bodega. He then meets up with DJ Tony Touch to discuss the many ways in which Latinos helped build Hip-Hop culture. Later, he visits the Puerto Rican Traveling Theatre to discuss the lack of access Latinos have to Broadway stages. He then shines a light on Luar, a fashion brand created, owned and operated by Raul Lopez, a true kid from the barrio, who unlike so many Latino designers before him is getting the recognition he deserves. John also pays tribute to salsa music and the people who keep it alive throughout the city. Lastly, John hosts one of his “Legends Dinners” with the help of Colombian chef, Stephanie Del Carmen who focuses on cooking and honoring indigenous food. Food that John, comedian Aida Rodriguez, DJ Tony Touch, Fat Joe and Broadway star Sergio Trujillo enjoy while discussing all things Latin.

programme.title: Leguizamo Does America
programme.desc: John tours Miami's historic sector, Little Havana, with community leader Marvin Tapia, and discusses the influence of the Cuban community in Miami. He then meets with the Borges family who through hard work and sacrifice have turned their dreams of owning a Cuban Coffee shop into a reality. John later meets with actor, playwright and activist Carmen Pelaez to discuss the stronghold the right wing has had on the Latino community and the future of Miami politics. John then visits the arts district of Miami which has earned that title largely due to the Latinx artists who have painted its impressive walls. He speaks with Evoca1, a muralist whose ideology has influenced him to tell complex Latinx stories through his art. Later, John meets up with singer/songwriter Elena Rose to discuss the current state of Latin music, Miami as its hub, and the future for her and other women in the industry. John also visits Peruvian chef Juan Chipoco at his flagship restaurant, Ceviche 105 to learn about his journey to becoming a mogul in the industry, by honoring the food of his homeland. Lastly, John meets up with comedian George Harris, who has built community and gives immigrants a safe place to laugh and rejoice in stories that reflect their personal experiences.

programme.title: Leguizamo Does America
programme.desc: John first meets up with actor Diane Guerrero to chat about her experiences as an immigrant, author and activist over some of the best Latin food in town. He then meets up with Congressman Ritchie Torres from the Bronx to discuss the lack of Latinx representation in government and what can be done about it. John also speaks with Maria Teresa Kumar of Voto Latino to discuss the importance of voter registration within the Latinx community and how that will increase representation. And he goes canvassing to register potential voters with the help of activist and Vice President of Unidos US, Clarissa Martinez De Castro. John then takes a tour of the Smithsonian's Latin American History exhibition with Founding Director of The National Museum of the American Latino, Jorge Zamanillo to discuss the need for a proper museum that more accurately tells the Latinx story in this country. Later, John meets up with poet/activist Quique Aviles, who has lived in D.C. since he was a child and fights against gentrification through the spoken word. Lastly, John visits the Gala Theater and the immigrant kids who find their voices and their strength through performance art.

programme.title: Leguizamo Does America
programme.desc: John meets up with renowned actor Michael Peña to learn about what it was like to grow up in the Midwest, while still attempting to retain his Mexican culture. By participating in a Peña family barbeque, John sees first-hand just how much family and friends who become family, help keep traditions alive and create safe spaces for those making a new home in a strange land. John also meets up with Congressman Chuy Garcia, who works tirelessly to bring about equity in government on behalf of the Latinx community. John then connects with Mario Hernández Jr. who shows him around the borough of Pilson, where political murals (a tradition started in Mexico) line the streets. He then talks with local artists who are passing on the tradition of political art to the community through printmaking. John has a chat with some of Chicago's queer youth activists who are actively working to provide education and a safe environment for their peers. Lastly, John gets the lowdown on Humboldt Park from community leader, Mike Oquendo. He introduces John to the Humboldt Park firemen who are entirely Latino and break all stereotypes through their community activism.

programme.title: Duke Ellington
programme.sub-title: Duke Ellington: Jazz from Newport, Brussels, 1973
programme.desc: In 1956, Duke Ellington and his Orchestra performed a legendary set at the third annual Newport Jazz Festival. It was tenor saxophonist Paul Gonsalves' outstanding 27-chorus solo on “Diminuendo and Crescendo in Blue” that revitalized Ellington's career. The success generated during that performance carried him for the rest of his life. By 1973, festivals carrying the Newport name were organized all over the world. Less than a year before his death, Ellington and his Orchestra, with Gonsalves still in the fold, appeared in Brussels to deliver a timeless performance before a highly appreciative crowd.

programme.title: Robert Glasper & Jason Moran
programme.sub-title: La Gaité Lyrique: Robert Glasper & Jason Moran
programme.desc: As part of the Blue Note Jazz Festival and Blue Note Records' 75th anniversary, pianists Robert Glasper and Jason Moran delivered a stunning jazz piano duet, resulting in an exciting exploration of the great history of jazz. An intricate knowledge of African-American music is infused with modern energy. The two musicians reinterpret the stride piano tradition through captivating original songs and personal interpretations of the music of Albert Ammons, Andrew Hill, Thelonious Monk, Ornette Coleman and Herbie Hancock.

programme.title: Christian Scott
programme.desc: In 2019, the vibrant city of Sofia, Bulgaria, hosted the International A to JazZ Festival. Among the featured acts was the trailblazing jazz trumpeter Christian Scott aTunde Adjuah. This genre-defying artist captivated the audience with his eclectic “stretch music” an approach that incorporates hip-hop and reaches back through the American canon into African percussion and its rich melodic content. Trumpeter Christian Scott aTunde Adjuah's quintet, which includes drummer Corey Fonville, percussionist Weedie Braimah, pianist Lawrence Fields, and bass guitarist Max Mucha, delivered a performance that defied boundaries and expectations.

programme.title: Popa Chubby
programme.sub-title: Popa Chubby - It's A Mighty Hard Road
programme.desc: Popa Chubby, born as Ted Horowitz, is an American blues singer and guitarist, who has become one of the most popular figures of New York's blues scene. Early on in his career he released the album 'Booty and the Beast' (1995), which became one of the 1990s's best-selling blues albums. Chubby describes his own style as "The Stooges meets Buddy Guy, Motörhead meets Muddy Waters, and Jimi Hendrix meets Robert Johnson". As part of the French music festival Blues sur Seine, Chubby and his band played a great blues rock concert, showcasing the artist's powerful and soulful guitar playing. This performance was recorded at Théâtre Simone Signoret on November 8, 2019.

programme.title: Thelonious Monk
programme.desc: An extraordinary collection of clips in all jazz genres from the most beautiful concerts and the most special festivals, performed by the finest jazz musicians.

programme.title: James Brown
programme.sub-title: James Brown - The North Sea Jazz
programme.desc: The North Sea Jazz Festival is the largest indoor music festival in the world, known globally as the event where the past, present and future of jazz are featured within three days. Next to a firm base of jazz as the festival's staple music genre, many others, such as blues, soul, funk, or hip hop, pass by. Back in 1981, legendary American singer James Brown gave an unforgettable performance at the North Sea Jazz Festival in The Hague. The "Godfather of Soul", "Mr. Dynamite", and "Soul Brother No. 1" was a major force in 20th century popular music, influencing many musicians in numerous genres.

programme.title: Wycliffe Gordon
programme.sub-title: Wynton Marsalis: In This House, on This Morning
programme.desc: The final concert of the Münchner Klaviersommer 1992 was by Wynton Marsalis and his septet. The 31-years-young star trumpeter performed one of his own jazz suites to the sold-out Philharmonie, under the title of ‘In This House, On This Morning'. The concert covers every jazz style, in composition and improvisation, and in both small and grand forms. Marsalis's Septet was made up of Wess Anderson (alto saxophone), Wycliffe Gordon (trombone), Reginald Veal (bass), Herlin Riley (drums), Todd Williams (tenor saxophone), and Eric Reed (piano).

programme.title: Herbert Blomstedt & Gewandhausorchester Leipzig
programme.sub-title: Symphonies in D by Mozart and Voríšek
programme.desc: Conductor Herbert Blomstedt and the Gewandhausorchester take us on a musical journey to the Czech Republic in this 2020 concert from Leipzig's Gewandhaus. The program opens with Bohemian composer Jan Václav Hugo Voríšek's Symphony in D major, Op. 23 (1821). Although the composition – Voríšek's only symphony – was never performed during the composer's lifetime, it has become one of his most-performed works today. Also on the program is Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart's Symphony No. 38 in D major, K. 504. This work, which premiered while Mozart was visiting Prague in 1787, is often referred to as the ‘Prague Symphony'.

programme.title: Seong-Jin Cho
programme.desc: Cinematic classical clips and energizing concert pieces featuring up-and-coming artists.

programme.title: Ivan Fischer, Budapest Festival Orchestra & Richard Goode
programme.sub-title: Beethoven - Symphonies and Piano Concerto No. 4
programme.desc: Iván Fischer leads his Budapest Festival Orchestra (BFO) in a concert program dedicated to the music of Ludwig van Beethoven. The program opens with the composer's Symphony No. 1 in C major, Op. 21. Afterward, Fischer conducts Beethoven's Piano Concerto No. 4 in G major, Op. 58, in which Richard Goode features as the soloist. The acclaimed American pianist (1943) is known for his interpretations of the Beethoven repertoire. Goode recorded all five Beethoven piano concertos with the BFO and Fischer, earning him exceptional critical acclaim and a Grammy Award nomination. As an encore, Goode performs the Sarabande from J. S. Bach's Partita in B-flat major, BWV 825. The program closes with Beethoven's famous Symphony No. 5 in C minor, Op. 67. This performance was recorded at the Béla Bartók National Concert Hall in Budapest, Hungary, on January 29, 2017.

programme.title: Les Dissonances
programme.desc: Cinematic classical clips and energizing concert pieces featuring up-and-coming artists.

programme.title: Mademoiselle Karen, Jakob Munch, Danish National Symphony Orchestra & Emma Smith
programme.desc: One night only! Welcome to the spectacular and decadent world of The Babylon Hotel, where music pours out of every crevice like bubbling champagne. In this concert, the upper class meets the underworld in a melting pot of euphoria and extravagance, nostalgia and pleasure with a carefree sinfulness only seen in the exuberant nightlife of the 1920s around the world. The concert features iconic music from movies and series, such as The Great Gatsby, Burlesque, and Babylon Berlin. The music is performed by the Danish National Symphony Orchestra and DR Big Band under the direction of Miho Hazama. The concert also features the Moka Efti Orchestra, and singers Madame le Pustra, Emma Smith, Mademoiselle Karen, Jakob Munch, and Nikko Weidemann, as well as dancers of Sweet Burlesque. This performance was recorded at the DR Koncerthuset in Copenhagen, Denmark, in May 2023.

programme.title: Ludwig Wicki, Danish National Symphony Orchestra, DR Junior Choir & Andrea Lykke Oehlenschlæger
programme.desc: Swiss conductor Ludwig Wicki leads the Danish National Symphony Orchestra and the DR Junior Choir in a sparkling concert program of film music. The program features a selection of music from great and Oscar-winning Hollywood films, including Titanic, The Bodyguard, Pirates of the Caribbean, Braveheart, and Moulin Rouge, written by major composers such as John Williams, Hans Zimmer, James Horner, and Klaus Badelt. Highlights include ‘He's a pirate/Davy Jones' from Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Man's Chest ‘Now We Are Free' from Gladiator ‘My Heart Will Go On' from Titanic and ‘Shallow' from A Star Is Born. Featured soloists in this concert are the young talented singers Andrea Lykke Oehlenschlæger and Diluckshan Jeyaratnam. This performance was recorded at the DR Koncerthuset in Copenhagen, Denmark, in May 2022.

programme.desc: Americana is American Roots music it incorporates everything from Blues to Country Music, to Zydeco, to Bluegrass, Appalachian Music, Folk. programme.title: Bluegrass
programme.desc: Bluegrass music is a form of American Roots music. It originates from the Appalachian Mountain regions of America, and it very much features acoustic instruments acoustic guitar, mandolin, banjo, and fiddle. It's music from struggle, but it's music, which has this sense of joy, and when it gets going, there's an almost religious fervour.

programme.title: Diana Ross & The Supremes
programme.desc: Diana Ross & Supremes- America's most successful female vocal group of all time was formed by four Detroit schoolgirls in the late 50s. Diana Ross (26 March 1944, Detroit, Michigan, USA), Betty McGlown (b. 1943, Detroit, Michigan, USA), Florence Ballard (b. 30 June 1943, Rosetta, Mississippi, USA, d. 22 February 1976, Detroit, Michigan, USA) and Mary Wilson (b. 6 March 1944, Greenville, Mississippi, USA) named themselves the Primettes in tribute to the local male group, the Primes - who themselves found fame in the 60s as the Temptations.

programme.title: Lenny Kravitz
programme.desc: American singer, songwriter, multi-instrumentalist, record producer, actor and arranger, whose "retro" style incorporates elements of rock, blues, soul, R&B, funk, jazz, reggae, hard rock, psychedelic, pop, folk and ballads. In addition to singing lead and backing vocals, Kravitz often plays all the guitar, bass, drums, keyboards, and percussion himself when recording. He won the Grammy Award for Best Male Rock Vocal Performance four years in a row from 1999 to 2002, breaking the record for most wins in that category as well as setting the record for most consecutive wins in one category by a male.

programme.title: Don't Spray Me with That
programme.desc: First, Sonia and Froy repossess an experimental concept motorcycle from a hose-wielding yuppie. Then, Froy and Sonia head to a casting agency to repossess a car from a two-faced receptionist with a special talent for four-letter words. And finally, Froy and Matt get way more than they bargained for when they try to repossess a van from an exterminator who is not afraid to unload his bug-killing spray on humans.

programme.title: Hidden Cars and Higher Powers
programme.desc: First, Matt and Froy get some help from a friendly gardener that lead them to discover a car hidden in the backyard. Next, Matt and Froy go on another repo to repossess from a preacher that believes it would be blasphemy to repo his car. And finally, cops arrive in the middle of what may be Matt and Froy's last repo. Lou has to swoop in and smooth out the situation.

programme.title: Repo, the Final Frontier
programme.desc: First, Froy and Sonia trace an out-of-state skip account to a seedy motel, where they come face-to-face with a hopping-mad cowboy and his female companion. Then, Matt and Sonia go where no repo man has gone before to repossess a Ford from a delusional nerd who may as well actually be from space. And finally, Lou and Lyndah have trouble getting to their car because there's a chainsaw-wielding gardener in the way.

programme.title: Mess with Texas
programme.desc: Matt and Froy face incredible odds against a Texas-sized biker gang looking for a beat down, and when bullets fly, it starts to look like they may never come back home. Back in LA, Frankie's wild antics jeopardize a repo and push Lyndah to her breaking point.

programme.title: Don't You Dare Lift-off
programme.desc: First, Froy, Matt, and Lou turn tail and bail when bats and guns are swung towards them. Later, things get heated for Matt and Sonia just before they head out with Esteban and Lou to ambush a helicopter pilot.

programme.title: Doomsday Prep
programme.desc: The team wonders what's up with Sonia as she wrestles with Lou's big secret. Ronnie goes undercover and takes a gold digging RO out on a date, but when Carlos lets the cat out of the bag all hell breaks loose.

programme.title: Intoxicated Marina Idiots
programme.desc: First, Lou and Sonia are on a hunt for a 2007 Porsche & a 2008 Audi from the same drug dealing debtor. Next, Matt and Froy pry a truck from a group of intoxicated idiots at a marina. And finally, Matt and Lou hit up an upscale Glendale neighborhood to scoop up a BMW from a shoe throwing rich couple.

programme.title: Disaster at the Derby
programme.desc: The team gets back to work, but have their anger management sessions had any effect? Sonia and Lyndah find themselves surrounded by a gang of roller derby girls who've got the skills to protect their car, Lou and Sonia get lost in translation when they're forced to contend with a pair of Russian movers and their yuppie lawyer clients, and Matt, Lou, and Froy are in a battle of the scams when they try to trick a group of con artists into giving up their vehicle.

programme.title: Probation Abomination
programme.desc: Sonia returns, but not before putting the team on probation and laying some ground rules.

programme.title: The Road to Recovery
programme.desc: On this premiere of Operation Repo's brand new season, Sonia's rehabilitation from her gunshot wound takes a shocking turn. The whole team runs into trouble on a job when they attempt to repossess a carnival ride from a group of wannabe carnies, Froy and Matt wrangle with a gym owner and his hired muscle, and Lou, Froy, and Sonia repo a truck from a protester in quite the revealing outfit.

programme.title: Off the Hitch
programme.desc: In this very special episode of Operation Repo, it's the 36 hours that change everything. When Matt, Froy, and Lyndah go on a routine repo gone violently wrong, the bonds that connect the Pizarro family may be torn apart forever.

programme.title: Unwanted Seizures
programme.desc: First, Lou sends Sonia and Matt to repo a Toyota Corolla from a recently divorced debtor determined to keep the car she won in the divorce. Later, Lou cuts a repo short to send Froy and Matt to tail a car until the debtor realizes he's being followed. And Finally, when a debtor goes into a seizure during his seizure of his vehicle, Lou, Sonia, and Lyndah tend to the man while they wait for help to arrive.

programme.title: Breakdowns and Beatdowns

programme.title: Above the Dolomites
programme.desc: Soar up and over Italy's iconic mountain range for a full hour of inspiration and relaxation with "Above the Dolomites." Featuring cinematography by new collaborator Ryan Shirley, and synchronized to new healing music by Antonio Resende, it offers some simply stunning views that showcase just why the Dolomites might be the world's most epic mountain range.

programme.title: Tropical Weightlessness
programme.desc: Gently sway in the tropical turquoise waters with a glimpse of blue sky and palm trees.

programme.title: Rainbow Reef Relaxation 2
programme.desc: Sit back, relax and enjoy the dazzling world of coral reefs in 4K you embark on a journey through some of the most vibrant underwater reefs on our blue planet - including many scenes which no longer look like this due to coral bleaching. From the cinematographer: "The footage for this film was shot over a period of some years in French Polynesia, Micronesia, Indonesia and East Timor. Unfortunately there are not that vibrant anymore today. Coral reefs have suffered from the rise of water temperature, rise in acidity, destructive fishing methods and severe storms caused by climate change." - Peter Schneider. "Rainbow Reef Relaxation" is a 3-hour long, sweeping yet majestic & vibrant underwater journey to the world's most beautiful lagoons, as captured by Nature Relaxation collaborator Peter Schneider (Underwatercam) paired with ambient underwater sounds and brand new ambient music by the Cynic Project. Filmed in High Definition several years ago, thanks to its age this film offers magical glimpses of an underwater world that is bursting with life, color, and beauty.
